anti_claim: Organ registry rows are public runtime authority metadata and receipt inventory only; accepted_current_authority status, organ counts, adapter-backed counts, and generated receipt counts do not create score-based progress, release, product completeness, private-root equivalence, source mutation, proof correctness, whole-system correctness, provider-call, secret-export, or trading/financial-advice authority.

overread_guard: accepted_current_authority names current public receipts only; it is not product progress, release readiness, proof correctness, or source authority above macro contracts
organ counts and adapter-backed counts are inventory fields, not maturity, completeness, benchmark, or launch gates
generated_receipt_count points to public-relative receipt files only; it is not proof authority, private-root equivalence, or complete secret detection

Every accepted organ row carries evidence_class, evidence_strength_rank, claim_ceiling, truth_accounting_bucket, and counts_as_real_substrate_progress; read status through those fields before inferring evidence strength.
authority_ceiling.reader_action: Use implemented_organs to find current public runtime targets, runner commands, and receipt refs; do not treat accepted_current_authority, organ counts, adapter-backed counts, or generated receipt counts as product progress, release readiness, proof correctness, private-root equivalence, or whole-system authority.
registry_can_claim: which public target organs have current-authority acceptance receipts
which commands regenerate their public receipts
which generated receipts are public-relative and body-redacted
that a bounded public Lean/Lake witness compiled in a temporary workspace
registry_cannot_claim: Lean/Lake proof authority beyond the bounded witness
macro source authority above current reconstruction receipts
uniform accepted_current_authority without per-organ evidence strength

evidence_class_registry.reader_action: Read accepted_current_authority together with each row evidence_class, claim_ceiling, and truth_accounting_bucket; status alone is receipt inventory, not evidence strength.

implemented_organs.classification_basis: public source-faithful realization of the finite denominator-order certificate at the heart of the Erdos #257 period-noncollapse strike; the runnable exercise sums S_F(b)=sum 1/(b^n-1) as an exact Fraction, reads the reduced denominator Q, computes the multiplicative order of b mod Q by repeated multiplication, cross-checks Q against the closed form B/gcd(A_L,B), and confirms ord_Q(b)=lcm(F) over positive cases including a reducing case, while rejecting forged certificates (a wrong order or a wrong denominator) by recomputation and keeping solves-Erdos257, machine-checked-proof, oracle-or-prover, provider, release, and publication authority false

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Validates a bridge campaign fan-in DAG against a public-safe subset of the macro CR/VR rule families over bounded public fixtures (schema and identity, unique node labels, existing dependency edges, acyclicity, exactly one synthesis node reaching a probe, barrier alignment, and provider safe-parallelism) and rejects malformed campaigns by recomputation; does not dispatch agents, execute campaigns, prove provider correctness, authorize release or publication, or claim full private-root equivalence.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: public source-faithful realization of the bridge campaign fan-in DAG validator; the runnable exercise validates each campaign spec against a public-safe subset of the macro CR/VR rule families - schema and identity, unique node labels, existing dependency edges, DFS acyclicity, exactly one synthesis node reaching a probe, barrier alignment, and provider safe-parallelism - accepting two well-formed campaigns (a linear chain and a three-way fan-in) and rejecting a cyclic campaign (CR012) and a two-synthesis campaign (CR013) by recomputation, while keeping dispatch, multi-agent execution, provider-safety-proof, release, and publication authority false

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Exercises a synthetic SQLite durable-queue capsule over bounded public fixtures: an active-idempotency uniqueness guard, lease claim and expired-lease recovery to a recoverable state, a blackboard claim-event projection where a contradiction invalidates an assertion, and a cold-start reconciliation taxonomy over the job/run/log triple. It rejects inconsistent store states by recomputation, asserting the expected reconciliation rule id fires. It does not ship the private live metabolism database, does not dispatch agents or call providers, does not auto-repair ambiguous state, is not a distributed database, and does not authorize release or publication.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: Classified bounded_runtime_computation because each case actually executes the capsule's real runtime: it stands up a temp SQLite store, runs DDL/inserts/updates, performs lease math against the process clock, projects claim events, and runs the reconciler over the resulting rows. This is genuine bounded computation over a synthetic store, not a fixture echo (output is not pre-baked in the fixture), not a schema replay (it mutates and reads real SQLite state), and not an external subprocess/semantic-validator (no external binary, no NL judgement). Negatives are rejected by recomputation: the reconciler derives the firing rule from observed job/run/log state, and substituting a wrong expected marker flips the organ to fail.

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Exercises a registry-backed derived fact provider over bounded public fixture roots: it resolves json_pointer (RFC 6901, including list-index traversal), glob_count (with excluded prefixes), and named callable facts (git-tracked counts), and it turns provider failures into error-as-data rows that degrade the receipt status rather than crash the ledger. Positive cases must resolve to the expected values with a clean receipt; negative cases must be rejected by recomputation with the expected provider error_class firing on the planted-defect fact. It is not a doctrine truth auditor, not a full macro fact-registry export, not semantic claim validation, and it does not authorize release or publication.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: evidence_class=bounded_runtime_computation because the organ runs the real capsule computation end-to-end over authored fixtures with a deterministic, bounded workload: JSON-pointer resolution, glob enumeration, and a small git index built inside an isolated tempdir. It is not a mere fixture_echo (it recomputes values and compares observed vs expected, and falsifiability is demonstrated), not a schema_replay (it executes the provider logic, not just shape-checks), not an algorithmic_projection (no model/extrapolation), not an external_subprocess_witness in the gating sense (the git subprocess is an internal callable-fact detail over a throwaway tempdir, not the central claimed mechanism and not an absent dependency), and not a semantic_validator (it explicitly does NOT judge truth of claims). family_id=research_and_science_replays fits a registry/provider data-resolution runtime that replays authored fact-resolution cases with planted-defect rejection; it is closer to a research/replay data-evaluation organ than to entry, navigation, formal proof, agent-safety, import-projection, or work-landing families.

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Recomputes a curated route-packet navigation benchmark over bounded public fixtures: recall and precision of selected artifacts against expected stable ids, forbidden-first-route checks, scent-term coverage, latency status against a per-task budget, and sufficiency/latency debt candidates. Each case carries a planted expectation, and the runner accepts a case only when the recomputation matches it; planted negative cases are rejected by recomputation. It is not a live private kernel run, not an embedding benchmark, not a universal navigation benchmark, and not release authority.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: Classified as bounded_runtime_computation: the organ runs the real capsule's deterministic, stdlib-only benchmark recomputation (set-based recall/precision, substring forbidden-route check, scent-term coverage, integer latency-budget comparison, percentile summary) over small bounded fixture inputs at call time. It is not a mere fixture echo/replay (the verdict is computed, not stored), not an algorithmic projection (it executes the actual scoring code path on the inputs), and needs no external subprocess, prover, or network — so external_subprocess_witness / semantic_validator do not apply. Negatives are decided by recomputation contradicting a planted expectation, confirmed by two falsifiability probes.

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Ranks a sanitized in-memory annex catalog against a problem statement using explainable tiered weighted-token retrieval over bounded public fixtures: structured routing fields score highest, family text and open-first summaries weaker, curated notes weakest, with an exact/phrase/token-overlap score per tier and a per-row match breakdown. It rejects unroutable problems by recomputation, returning no_match when no candidate scores above zero or a filter excludes every candidate. It is not BM25, not TF-IDF, not embedding or semantic search, does not clone repositories, ships no private annex corpus, and is not a license, provenance, or release authority.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: evidence_class=bounded_runtime_computation because _evaluate_case invokes the real capsule function route_catalog (which transitively runs route_annexes) over each fixture and compares the live-computed status/slug/score/note-ids to fixture expectations — it is genuine deterministic computation over bounded inputs, not a fixture echo (the answers are recomputed, not read from the fixture) and not an external subprocess (pure in-process stdlib). family_id=architecture_and_navigation because the mechanism is a routing/navigation surface: it maps a problem statement onto ranked entries of a knowledge catalog (annex discovery/navigation), which is the architecture-and-navigation concern rather than math/proof, agent-reliability, or science-replay. gate_runnable=true: no absent dependency; build_result returns status=pass with 4 cases under PYTHONPATH=src python3.

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Audits a single agent-output text string against a phrase-membership egress self-compliance policy over bounded public fixtures: it fires a violation only when a tripwire phrase is present and the matching legitimiser phrase is absent across three detectors (permission-gate-without-blocker, self-error-without-capture, command-displacement-to-operator). It rejects policy-violating text by recomputation. It is phrase membership only: it does not perform taint analysis, prompt-injection defense, sandboxing, or information-flow proof, and it does not authorize release or publication.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: Classified bounded_runtime_computation because each case executes the capsule's real phrase-membership detection at runtime: it recomputes the three detector verdicts (permission-gate-without-blocker, self-error-without-capture, command-displacement-to-operator) over the agent-output text by testing tripwire-phrase presence against legitimiser-phrase absence, and compares the observed green/red verdict plus the firing diagnostic id to the case expectation. It is not a fixture echo or schema replay (the verdict is recomputed, proven by repair/break flips), not an external subprocess, and explicitly not semantic understanding; it is bounded deterministic computation over public fixtures. Negatives are rejected by recomputation: a planted policy-violating text fires its tripwire without a legitimiser and the gate flags it red with the expected diagnostic id.

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Exercises an owner-routed generated projection drift gate over bounded public fixtures: per-file SHA-256 fingerprinting of source authorities and artifacts, a prior-clean-receipt source-hash skip cache, required-artifact presence, and each owner's declared no-write check command return code. It reports a drifted owner by recomputation. It does not prove that every macro owner uses true content-diff semantics, does not repair files, does not validate the full macro registry, and does not authorize public release.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: evidence_class=external_subprocess_witness because the surfaced mechanism's drift verdict is a check-command return-code witness (the capsule's no-write check command, run through its command runner — the same path that would invoke subprocess.run for an external owner command), not a pure in-module computation. The two positives and two negatives each route through the capsule's real check pipeline; negatives are rejected by recomputation (planted byte -> check_command_failed via builtin:assert-file-equals; missing artifact -> artifact_missing presence check), and the cache-hit positive is proven to skip the command via the real source-hash cache (check_mode=source_hash_cache_hit, hit_count=1). gate_runnable=true because every fixture stays on the in-process builtin command surface, so no absent external binary is needed.

implemented_organs.claim_ceiling: Runs a symbolic Lean proof-search lab over bounded public toy theorems with the installed Lean subprocess: an and/or candidate-tactic search, a forward oracle-leak firewall, a problem-id ablation, and a #print axioms cleanliness gate. It rejects forged or memorising scenarios by recomputation. Lean is an optional dependency: when the lean binary is absent the organ is locked and verifies nothing rather than faking a pass. It is not neural theorem proving, does not solve any open mathematical problem, does not forward oracle proof bodies, and does not authorize release or publication.
implemented_organs.classification_basis: evidence_class=external_subprocess_witness because the surfaced mechanism's verdict is produced by the installed Lean subprocess: run_and_or_search and run_statement_only_hammer call check_candidate_with_lean which shells out to `lean` to compile each candidate tactic body, a #print axioms cleanliness gate rejects sorry-tainted candidates, and a forward oracle firewall plus a problem-id ablation reject leaked oracle bodies and policies that only work because they memorised the problem id. The positive fixture is accepted only when real Lean closes the toy theorems; the four negatives are rejected by recomputation (two oracle-firewall violations, one axiom taint, one problem-id ablation failure). It is a gated external-tool witness: when the lean binary is absent the organ returns a terminal locked state and verifies nothing rather than counting tool-absence as a mechanism failure or faking a pass.
